REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JUSTIN D'ORIO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS (CCP SECTION 438). THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO (1) VERIFY HIS COMPLAINT FOR QUIET TITLE; AND (2) ALLEGE FACTS, NOT CONCLUSIONS RE: DISCOVERY OF THE FACTS CONSTITUTING THE MISTAKE AS IN ARTHUR V. DAVIS (1981) 126 CAL.APP.3D 684, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO WHAT FRY THOUGHT HE WAS SIGNING; WHY HE THOUGHT SO; HOW THE GRANT DEED GOT RECORDED AND BY WHOM IF FRY DID NOT INTEND TO DELIVER IT; AND WHY HE DID NOT DISCOVER THE NATURE OF THE DOCUMENT HE HAS SIGNED EARLIER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RONALD QUIDACHAY, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED (501/REQ)

Motion And Notice Of Hearing On Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings (Ccp Section 438)
Off Calendar Jun-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JUSTIN D'ORIO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS (CCP SECTION 438)

Appearances: David Wasserman of Wasserman Stern (415.567.9600) for defendant and moving party; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to (1) verify his complaint for quiet title; and (2) allege facts, not conclusions re: discovery of the facts constituting the mistake as in Arthur v. Davis (1981) 126 Cal.app.3d 684, including but not limited to what Fry thought he was signing; why he thought so; how the Grant Deed got recorded and by whom if Fry did not intend to deliver it; and why he did not discover the nature of the document he has signed earlier. Order signed. Judge: Ronald Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)
